chain.thought: Explanation: The youngest age at the start date (when becoming the oldest living president) is 49 years, 241 days, which belongs to Theodore Roosevelt.

judge_thought: Explanation: Interpreting the question in context of the table as “Who was the youngest when becoming the oldest living president,” we compare the “age at start date” values. The smallest age listed is Theodore Roosevelt at 49 years, 241 days (June 24, 1908). All others started at older ages (e.g., Taft 51y 170d, Grant 53y 95d). Therefore, the Prediction Answer is correct.
